name: Syracuse University Pulp Covers Collection
description: Cover images from dime novels, pulp magazines, juvenile series books, fashion magazines, comics, science fiction and adventure stories. Broad sampling of popular literature cover art.

name: Museum fur Gestaltung Zurich Poster Collection (eMuseum)
description: Over 380,000 international posters from 1860 to present — political, commercial, cultural. One of the most extensive poster archives in the world. 100,000+ works searchable online.

name: German Poster Museum (Deutsches Plakat Museum)
description: 350,000 posters spanning political, business and cultural domains. Strongest in early German posters (1880–1914), Weimar Republic, GDR cultural posters, and reunified Germany. ~30,000 illustrated online.

name: People's Graphic Design Archive
description: Crowd-sourced virtual archive of inclusive graphic design history. 5,000+ contributions — finished projects, process work, photos, correspondence, oral histories. Browsable by color, year, format.

name: archives.design
description: Curated graphic design repository sourcing from Internet Archive. Organized by Annuals, Books, Branding, Editorial, Ephemera, Posters, Type Specimens. Vintage airline posters, IBM materials, type catalogs.

name: AIGA Design Archives
description: 20,000+ selections from AIGA national juried design competitions dating to 1924. Typography, illustration, editorial, advertising, posters. Browsable by color (120 options), year, format.

name: Harvard Daguerreotypes
description: 3,500+ daguerreotypes from across Harvard. Earliest successful moon photographs, first ether anesthesia operations, portraits of African-born slaves, early class albums. Works by Brady, Southworth & Hawes, Whipple.

name: George Eastman Museum Photography Collection
description: 400,000+ photographic objects from 1839 to present in all major processes — daguerreotype to digital. 8,000+ photographers represented. 250,000+ objects searchable online.

name: Old Book Illustrations
description: Public domain restored illustrations scanned from old books. Engravings, woodcuts, steel engravings. Browsable by technique (woodcut, steel engraving, lithograph), subject, artist. High-resolution downloads.

name: SFO Museum Aviation Collection
description: 162,000+ objects from commercial aviation history including airline travel posters, luggage labels, uniforms, and ephemera. 40,000+ objects accessible online. Strong in mid-century airline poster art.

name: Boston Public Library Travel Posters (Digital Commonwealth)
description: 350+ vintage travel posters from the Golden Age of Travel. Railways, ocean liners, automobile tourism. Vivid graphic styles promoting destinations worldwide. IIIF-compliant images.

name: National Fairground and Circus Archive
description: Largest public circus history collection in the UK. Thousands of posters, photographs, programmes, engravings, and ephemera covering fairs, circuses, and travelling shows. Partially digitized.

name: DigitaltMuseum (Nordic Collections)
description: Common database for 223 Norwegian and Swedish museums. 4+ million photographs, objects, artworks. Includes Nordiska museet's 6 million images of Nordic cultural heritage, folk art, textiles, daily life.

name: RIT Cary Graphic Arts Collection
description: Premier library on graphic communication history. Graphic Design Archive documents significant American graphic designers active 1920s–1960s (Saul Bass, Paul Rand, Herbert Bayer). Original artwork, sketchbooks, printed materials.

name: Smithsonian Seed & Nursery Catalog Collection
description: Covers from 250 of 10,000+ seed and nursery catalogs. Chromolithograph cover art featuring flowers, vegetables, fruits. Strong Burpee Collection. Documents horticultural advertising art evolution.

name: Princeton University Art Museum — Prints & Drawings
description: 20,000+ works on paper plus illuminated manuscripts. Strengths in Durer, Rembrandt, Goya prints; Italian Renaissance drawings; American watercolors by Homer, Hopper, O'Keeffe. 14th century to present.

name: UCLA Children's Book Collection
institution: UCLA Library Special Collections (via Internet Archive)
description: 1,800+ digitized children's fiction, poems, and educational books. Woodcut vignettes, color plates, illustrated covers. Searchable by year, topic, creator. Fairy tales, moral tales, adventure stories.

name: UW Historical Children's Literature Collection
description: 7,000+ titles spanning 17th–20th century. Alphabet books, primers, bestiaries, story books, fables. European and American books. Pan and zoom on high-resolution scans of illustrated pages.

name: American Folk Art Museum Collection
institution: American Folk Art Museum, New York
description: 1,200+ paintings, 1,500 drawings, 1,000 3D works (weathervanes, carousel animals, trade signs), 1,000 textiles (quilts, samplers, hooked rugs). Outsider art, self-taught art, folk traditions.

name: Calisphere (California Digital Library)
institution: University of California / California Digital Library
description: 2.2 million+ images from 300+ California institutions. Photographs, artwork, lithographs, daguerreotypes, advertisements, pictorial lettersheets. Strong in California history, Gold Rush era, and early state development.

name: National Library of Australia — Pictures Collection
description: Australia's visual heritage: paintings, drawings, watercolours, prints, photographs (daguerreotypes to digital), political cartoons, caricatures. Documents Australian life, environment, flora and fauna. Accessible via Trove (1.6M images).

name: NASA JPL Visions of the Future Posters
description: 14 retro-futuristic travel posters for exoplanets and solar system destinations. Art deco influenced graphic style by JPL's Studio team (9 artists). Free high-res downloads. Includes Mars Explorers Wanted and Exoplanet series.

name: NASA Image and Video Library
description: 140,000+ images, videos, and audio from across NASA missions. Apollo photography, Hubble imagery, spacecraft diagrams, mission patches, vintage NASA graphics. Downloadable in multiple resolutions with EXIF data.

name: Circusmuseum (Netherlands)
description: 7,300 posters, 7,900 photographs and postcards, 400 prints and engravings, 4,000 programs. European circus history documentation. Largely being digitised. Vivid chromolithograph circus poster art.

name: Pulp Magazine Archive
description: Thousands of digitized pulp magazines — Weird Tales, True Detective, Adventure, If, Captain Billy's Whiz Bang. Full scans with vivid painted covers. Science fiction, horror, detective, adventure genres.
